events.label: reasoning
events.text: The scenario is about a PVC expansion fitting omitted on a long exterior run. NEC Article 352 covers PVC conduit (RNC). Expansion fittings are required for PVC due to thermal expansion/contraction. NEC 352.44 covers expansion characteristics and requires expansion fittings where temperature changes are significant. This is a non-compliant condition.

    "PVC expansion fitting omitted on long exterior run, violating thermal expansion accommodation requirements",
    "Long straight PVC run exposed to temperature variation lacks required expansion/contraction control"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Install listed PVC expansion fitting(s) at appropriate intervals on the exterior run per NEC 352.44 and manufacturer instructions",
    "Calculate expected expansion/contraction based on temperature differential and run length to determine number and placement of expansion fittings",
    "Re-inspect after correction before closing in the work"
  ],
  "rationale": "PVC conduit has significant thermal expansion and contraction. NEC Article 352 requires expansion fittings where necessary to accommodate movement from temperature changes. Omitting an expansion fitting on a long exterior run is a non-compliant installation that can cause conduit buckling, joint separation, or damage to connected equipment.",
  "references": [
    "NEC Art. 352.44 (Expansion Characteristics)",
    "NEC Art. 352 (Rigid PVC Conduit)",
    "NEC Art. 344 (Rigid Metal Conduit - comparative reference)"
  ]
